name: Blue Cash Everyday® Card from American Express

verdict.text: No annual fee — an easy keep if you shop groceries, gas, or online

earn_note: Each 3% category caps at $6,000/yr in spend (max $180/yr per category, $540/yr across all three) before dropping to 1% — generous for a no-fee card, but track your spend if you're close to a cap.

points.note: Not a points program — cash back here is always worth exactly 1¢, redeemed as a statement credit. No transfer partners, no premium redemption to chase.

transfer_partners.highlight: Cash back isn't a transferable currency — it's a straight statement credit, always worth face value.

credits.name: Disney Bundle Credit
credits.subtitle: $7/mo
credits.description: Monthly statement credit toward a Disney Bundle subscription (Disney+, Hulu, ESPN+).
tips: Only worth it if you already subscribe to the Disney Bundle — otherwise it's $0 to you.

protection_note: Thin by design — this is a no-fee cash-back card, not a travel card. No trip delay, trip cancellation, baggage, or rental car coverage, and no extended warranty (that's reserved for the paid Blue Cash Preferred tier).
rental_note: No rental car coverage at all — use a different card for rentals.

services.name: Foreign transaction fee applies
services.detail: 2.7% on purchases made outside the US — unlike the premium cards in this dashboard, this one is not built for international use.

additional_cards.title: Additional cards

benefits.text: Earns cash back on their spend (into your account)
Same purchase protection as the primary card

additional_cards.note: Additional cards are free — no reason not to add a trusted household member.
